algebraic_symbol.rank_one_formula: D2W(F)[a tensor xi,a tensor xi]=|a tensor xi|^2+R|DM2(F)[a tensor xi]|^2+K|DM3(F)[a tensor xi]|^2

artifact: AP-E12 endpoint graph-density and relaxed-minimizer audit

checks.detail: M2 L2 needs alpha>1/2 while the forced endpoint gap needs alpha<1/2
checks.name: power-law Malý obstruction excluded by M2 L2

checks.detail: M2 L2 needs beta>1/4, forcing a(r)^4/r^2 to zero
checks.name: logarithmic borderline closes without overlap
rank-one discontinuity is approximable and not a counterexample

checks.detail: no finite-grid proxy promotes an endpoint density or continuum theorem
checks.name: theorem gates fail closed

endpoint_scaling.conclusion: the classical two-hole/dipole endpoint obstruction cannot coexist with the AP-E11 L2 second-minor bound; this excludes that mechanism but is not a general density theorem
endpoint_scaling.model: u(r,theta)=a(r) gamma(theta), where gamma is the two-hole Malý loop

quotient_observables.quadrature: each exact tetrahedral action contribution lumped equally to its four vertices
each exact tetrahedral action contribution lumped equally to its four vertices
each exact tetrahedral action contribution lumped equally to its four vertices

finite_grid_isolation_proxy.scope: finite-dimensional same-action basin evidence only; not a continuum isolation theorem

rank_one_discontinuity.conclusion: finite complete-minor energy does not imply continuity, so continuous-Cartesian approximation theorems are insufficient; the example itself is nevertheless graph-norm approximable
rank_one_discontinuity.map: u(r,z)=(cos(log log(e/r)),sin(log log(e/r)),0,0) around an axis
rank_one_discontinuity.property: finite W12 energy but no continuous representative on the axis; derivative rank one, hence M2=M3=0

strong_quasiconvexity.proof: all complete-minor cross terms vanish because periodic first, second, and third minors have their affine means

complete_minor_endpoint_density.reason: known Cartesian approximation gives exponent loss q<p; the endpoint p=q=2 requires an additional equiintegrability/extension theorem not supplied by ordinary W12 density

graph_norm_degree_continuity.consequence: a smooth target-valued graph recovery sequence is eventually in the same integer degree

theory.growth_obstruction: the autonomous density has 2-to-6 growth (q/p=3); available generic (p,q)-regularity theorems require substantially smaller growth ratio and do not cover a sphere constraint plus relaxation gap
theory.ordered_next_gate: prove a local endpoint recovery/equiintegrability lemma or prove L^(2+delta) higher integrability of the complete-minor vector for the selected minimizer; only then derive weak EL and epsilon regularity

ordinary_W12_density.does_not_control: strong L2 convergence of M2 and M3
ordinary_W12_density.reason: pi_2(S3)=0 removes the ordinary Bethuel obstruction

sufficient_supercritical_route.statement: if the complete-minor vector belongs locally to L^(2+delta) and the map is in the appropriate Cartesian closure, Malý approximation with q=2 gives graph-L2 recovery
